reviews.review_text: Bought it dirt cheap on a sale and would have paid more. It's a great game. I can't get my son away from it 😂

after a couple of act or reaching new region, its all the same gmaeplay.

then what the point????
no anticipation of improvement of gameplay.
ig only for LOTR fans, this is nice

Yeah i'm not going to touch this game ever again. At first it was fun, but it gets repetitive and unfairly difficult. One time I was fighting a normal captain and I saw 3 other captains with him, and on top of that they summoned more and more minions — you can't fight them, and if you did manage to catch a captain, you cannot brand him or anything because you have 1,000 minions surrounding you. But I'm very sure Shadow of War is wayyyyy better than this game.
5/10.
one of the greatest game ever made, but the gamplay system lock away by greedy corpo.
Very fun game that still holds up in the big 26, much less bloat than the new game of the saga, even tho both are fun

Even though they selfishly patented the Nemesis system, said had a game coming 2025 that was cancelled using the nemesis system, and they haven't used it anywhere else but Middle-earth™. They still pay for the patent even though they don't use it.

Good game.

Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor is a fun, stylish hack & slash that shines most when you’re carving through hordes of orcs. If you like the combat of the Batman: Arkham games and the traversal of Assassin’s Creed, this blends those ideas into a solid, easy-to-pick-up experience. The Nemesis System is the standout feature, adding personality and unpredictability to your enemies in a way that keeps things interesting. That said, the game does get repetitive, especially early on, and the world feels a bit limited with only two relatively small maps. It’s easy to burn through the main content quickly if you stay focused, and it can feel like it needed more depth and expansion overall. Still, it’s a lot of fun while it lasts, and watching your character grow stronger while taking on tougher orcs is genuinely satisfying. Not perfect, but definitely worth playing.

Avoid early game ground battles unless you're certain you can kill the orks before they call in endless reinforcements. That's my main gripe with the game. Start off trying to fight 6 guys, end up trying to fight 30 guys. And these fights can be LONG (several minutes to infinity). As opposed to Batman Arkham series where battles are over in a matter of seconds.

Only three hours in but wow this game is bland and disappointing. Big fan of the LOTR universe but the open world in this game is just too lifeless and the Uruks seem to have no sense of sight or awareness. The Nemesis system seems cool, and it's the only real redeeming factor early on.
really enjoyable game recommended to all assassian creed fans and lords of the ring fans

Yes this runs extremely well on Steam Deck and CachyOS. 


This game is like Assassin's Creed meets Batman meets Skrim's Enchanted Bow. The gameplay here is really what kept me going! With no spoilers the story is serviceable, granted I haven't yet played the second game. 

I love that you can skip splash screens by spamming buttons! I usually have to look up some launch command or go and delete the splash screen videos but not with this game!

I found the gameplay loop to be pretty repetitive. As a fantasy and LOTR fan, the lore didn't do enough to grab me and keep me invested. Also generally speaking the RPG aspect of being able to upgrade abilities and progress a skill trees doesn't appeal to me when implemented in an action adventure game, I don't believe it does enough to change or affect gameplay in a rewarding manner, i had the same takeaway with Horizon.

Great game! A little short though.

It took me 11 hours to beat the main storyline, which brought me 33% done with the game.

The combat reminded me of the Batman series with the counters and attacks, so it wasnt too hard when i needed to relearn the controls.

The story was great, with most of it tying back into the actual movie characters and lore.

It did have minor bugs but it was mostly smooth and even with my older computer, i was able to get max frames on low-medium settings.

I will say, if you are thinking of getting this game for the open world and mechanics alone, i would reccomend getting Middle Earth Shadow Of War instead, as it keeps all of the good mechanics and then some. But, if you want to get this for the story, i do recommend it.

Conclusion: Good game for story, graphics, and overall vibe. However, if you want a game like this but with better graphics, mechanics, and content, i would recommend getting the second installation of this series, Middle-Earth: Shadow Of War.
excelente gameplay, muy entretenido hay veces que me olvido del tiempo mientras juego

Great Game IMO, It does at times feel boring to play but the main missions are fun and the characters are fantasic. And do I even need to bring up the vengence system. Would give a 8.7/10 and is definently worth the 20 dollars.
Very addictive gameplay, once you get the hang of attacking and more attacks it becomes such a fun game to try to beat all the different leveled orcs. The nemesis system does indeed live to the hype.

I have played **Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor**  and so far it has been a really enjoyable experience.

The combat feels smooth and satisfying, especially if you like action games with stealth, sword fighting, and open-world exploration. The **Nemesis System** is the biggest highlight for me. It makes enemy captains feel personal because they remember you, come back stronger, and create their own stories during gameplay.

The world has a dark and gritty Middle-earth atmosphere, and even after only a few hours, the game gives you a strong sense of progression. Unlocking new abilities, hunting captains, and taking revenge on enemies feels very rewarding.

The story is interesting, but the gameplay is definitely the main reason to play. If you enjoy games like Assassin’s Creed, Batman Arkham combat, or open-world action games, then this is easy to recommend.

**Recommended for players who enjoy action, stealth, fantasy worlds, and satisfying combat.**

Shadow of Mordor is amazing, whether you're a fan of LotR or just fantasy in general. Combat in this game is extremely fun, allowing for the player to string together satisfying combos and finishers. This is the first game I've played where I have actually found enjoyment in trying to complete all the different side quests (mainly because brutally gutting and beheading orcs never gets old.)
awesome great wow omg amazing i love the game game game game game game game game pogi ko i love the lord of the rings hahdgfajgkjagfghisjouhafgjafagha nyeh

This game is so nice I bought it twice. I played it on xbox back when it first released and now after my first gaming laptop, I purchased it again with an amazing steam sale. It quickly became my comfort game. The Nemesis system is amazing, the story is nice, but the game play is what kept me coming back to it. 8/10 would recommend.

The best part, hands down, is the combat. It takes that fluid Arkham style and makes it incredibly brutal. Pairing that with the Nemesis System was genius. Having random Orc captains remember you, get promoted because they killed you, and develop specific fears made every fight feel personal. Honestly, just messing with the Orc hierarchy is where the real game is. 

The story starts out with a cool, dark premise, but it definitely drops the ball by the end. That final "boss fight" being a glorified quick-time event was such a massive letdown after spending hours building up to it.
Overall? It’s totally worth playing just to hack and slash through Mordor and mess with the Orcs. Good game a solid 7 out of 10 type game.

Once you get the hang of it, this game is quite entertaining. It gets a bit repetitive at times, but this helps keep the game enjoyable and not overly tense. The fight scenes are amazing for such an old game.

You'll do some repeated missions, but the combat and some of the other mechanics are great, so it's safe to say this game is for me. Great story, great environment (especially for LOTR fans). Short story short: it's worth playing
Love shadow of mordor. A great hack and slash with a decent story. If you like Lord of the rings this is a great game.

Basically Arkham City in Middle-Earth.


The Nemesis system is cool and, though he game is obviously showing its age, is still a delight to play twelve years after it released. Do be warned: If you were expecting an epic hack n' slash adventure this game is more stealth oriented much like the aforementioned Arkham City so keep that in mind.
Uninstall !!, The game is way too difficult for me, especially the boss fights and the minions that protect them and keep spawning endlessly. Also, the controls are terrible...

I expected to be disappointed because every time there is an adaptation of a franchise of this magnitude it doesn't end well. But I have been taken aback! Even though the game isn't a masterpiece, I enjoyed the story and the playability (killing captains, lol). It's hard for a game's story to become an obsession and make you wonder what could happen in the next cinematic the way the films do, but I think it fulfilled its purpose.
Good story line and abilities. Can be repetitive however there is a lot of things to do before completing the game.

It's a good game, even though I can say I am having a hard time with it. I am a pretty casual gamer and don't quite have the necessary reflexes to win all battles (although I am getting better). I really like the fact that things will happen if you don't intervene in them, i.e. you have to make careful decisions and not lose too many battles to avoid being unable to prevent your enemies getting stronger! Love the graphics as well. Not too sure about the backstory, but that's a fairly minor gripe.

Actual playtime is much lower, I've left it on a bunch while working from home lol - it's a pretty short game actually. Definitely a bit of a nostalgia blast, but it's a total power-fantasy, very fun and simple style of gameplay with great fight sequences. IMO the Nemesis System is a bit overhyped, or at least isn't balanced the best to flesh it out since you become a damn near demigod with a few abilities unlocked, but it's very fun nonetheless.

Game is great, controls is technical but simple to adapt to it. Storyline is meh and bosses is like you're fighting against regular enemies but totally cool and impress with the amout of control and power you're holding as a main character which some games in 2026 needs WB really did well with this game but the 2nd game is even better and worth every grind. Highly recomend to players who likes the grind and reward system.

This was ahead of its time!!!
Hot take... the first game is better than the second. Just lacks replay value.

Still a great game after all those years, similar to the Arkham games, lots of stuff to explore and ways to delet your enemies, getting to experience the nemesis system was great, hopefully other games get it as well, a great buy during steam sale

an interesting take on possible events taking place just before sauron returned to power in middle earth. the combat and other gameplay mechanics are very similar to A creed so if you like that you will enjoy this. And if you enjoy lotr lore this game uses it well with only some creative liberties. Though they might have given celabrimbor a personality more befitting of Fayinor. over all 4 out of 5 from me if you care about my opinion. it loses that one point because the final duel could have had a bit more to it. As it mostly boils down to a bullet time event which doesn't care about the learned gameplay mechanics at all. though there are some other boss battles that make you use those. and the dlc use the mechanics really well too. if you play those you will feel like you got an extra game and a half out of them.
Controls much like the Batman Arkham games, plenty of opportunity to run around and delete orcs. Watch out on the stealth missions tho, you will fail for pretty much no reason
Never Played it until now, its great, if you love LOTR and want a nice open world game with cool nemesis system and not so bloated then its for you would recomend 10/10
Parkour and orcs. Must I say more. I would call this game an OG Assassin's Creed but Lord of the Rings. Have lots of hours logged on console.

splendiferously easy but if you let yourself lose a little bit the moments of aura in this game are unmatched, the base gameplay is fun but nothing beats seeing your bitter rival turn a hard fight into a 2v1 because he hates the other guy more

The gameplay is fun, but don't expect it to be lore-friendly, because it definitely is not. Also, Warner Bros are kinda ♥♥♥♥♥ for patenting the Nemesis System, which I'd love to see other games be able to imitate.
Great game. Talion is pretty much the Star Killer of the Lotr universe, though second game is better i still highly recommend Shadow of Mordor as it's still very good.
It feels like Batman, but set in Mordor. I like the gameplay mechanics, but I feel the story is a bit weak. Regardless, I enjoyed it.
This is basically Assassin's Creed in Middle Earth. But it is good. Sometimes repetetive. But overall, very solid.

This game mechanics are mostly simple gameplay, fun, and satisfying.
Don't get overwhelmed with the endless Captains wars, proceed through the story and come back and forth to the gangs wars. It's really fun, enjoyable and the story is nice.

I enjoyed the game more than The Withcer 3. and it does feel a good replacement for "crimson desert" if you want fun combat mostly. Not much to exploration here though.

Worth the money on a discount for it's really old game, more than 25 hours in the game and I'm really happy with its simple fight mechanics, and also some gameplay twists as you progress through the main story.
If you are a fan of The Lord of The Rings and you enjoy cutting the heads off Orcs, then this is the game for you! After, go play the next one!

What could have been...
An enjoyable game, arguably better than the sequel

Honestly Its just okay.... Not cannon tolkien lore but cool to see a story with celebrimbor. Combat is what held this game together just being satisfying as ♥♥♥♥. But my god it can be a slough to get through, and the end game boss fights suck balls, you will be disappointed. If you like LOTR just give it a whirl if its on sale only 35.6% of people beat this game fully. (As of writing this) Also as a final note that I can think of is the lore and story got confusing at times, some missions really didn't need to exist. Just like the sequel.

fluid, fun combat. branding the 5 warchiefs for the final mission is cancer though.
Controls are clunky, and you literally cant even complete a side quest at the start cause apparently in the time it takes to stealth kill an entire camp, the entire camp respawns while i sprint???

Game is very short for the price and expect to 100% the game and all DLC in about 25-30 hrs. Gameplay is very simple and repetitive with the DLC content being lazy additions to the base game that feel far inferior to the base game. Also the graphics are very dated with an absolute max cap of only 100 fps and the game is riddled with blurring effects which probably weren't even remotely needed when this game came out. I'd only recommend it on sale.

- Cool graphics, character movements, kill animations
- Good skill point tree development
- Interesting story

One of the best fighting mechanics I have ever played. You get to Kill armies of Orks and have huge amount of fun while doing it. Especially if you're a Tolkien fan.

ncredible Tactical Action!
I went in knowing nothing about the lore and I’m hooked. The Nemesis System is brilliant—it turns every encounter into a tactical puzzle rather than just a button-masher. Combat feels fast, fluid, and satisfying, especially when you pull off those stun-flurry combos. It’s challenging, but that makes taking down a Warchief feel like a true victory. Highly recommended!
Very different combat style than what i am used to, but i love the progression of abilities and hunting down captains.

This was so much fun and I definitely had a great time playing this.

[h3]The Positives :[/h3]
+ Combat starts slow and somewhat clunky, but it gets much better and fun as you unlock skills, and becomes even more fun once you unlock [spoiler]the brand ability.[/spoiler]
+ Satisfying weapon SFX, attacks and visceral finishers.
+ The world is alive with things reacting to the environment, like [spoiler]prisoners attacking uruks when you knock them down, monsters attacking uruks, uruks fighting amongst each other, etc.[/spoiler]
+ Nice graphics (just download the HD texture pack, it uses less than 4GB vram).
+ Nemesis system (shame they patented it).
+ "Lord of the Hunt" extra game mode is a lot of fun.
+ You get 4 trading cards for playing the game.
+ [spoiler]Gollum[/spoiler] is in the game!

[h3]The negatives :[/h3]
- The main story's last two major bosses' [spoiler]fights are absolutely underwhelming. The first major boss fight was actually good.[/spoiler]
- "The Bright Lord" extra game mode is bad; just skip it in my opinion and watch a gameplay of it, it's basically [spoiler]playing in a handicapped mode with the useful skills not available and only 3 rune slots per weapon. And since this mode is about controlling uruks, all the missions will require you to infiltrate their strongholds, and with some skills -that are useful in such situations- not available, you'll just suffer through this mode.[/spoiler]
- The Outcast liberation missions are repetitive and are all the same (rescue 3 prisoners). I would recommend you just do a couple for the money to upgrade your stats unless you want to 100%.
- Most of the weapons missions are repetitive (especially the bow) but not as bad as the Outcast liberation.
- The game does not look like the screenshots on the game page since those are edited with crepuscular rays, color enhancements, etc.
- Sometimes input registration gets bugged or is just inconsistent for [spoiler]brutalizing from above and Caragor's bite attacks.[/spoiler]
- No option to change FOV in-game.
- Cannot rebind menu controls.

[h3]Additional notes :[/h3]
! FPS is capped at 100 fps.
! The game now features all previously released content.
! The game is often on sale, so pick it up during one.

If you can see past the opening 1 to 3 hours as you get the hang of combat, you're in for a very fun game.
I recommend!
I thought this would be more ARPGish; instead it's stealth-heavy with infinite respawning enemies, so you can't properly clear an area. Inputs also feel dopey and delayed.

Nope, 

- forced controller mapping that outright sucks, worst button combinations i've experienced.
- No field of view or hud disabling options
- stealth arrow doesnt even count as "stealth" so why fkn name it as such?!
- awful low resolution HUD and user interface, and cinematics are so blurred that you cant see wtf is happening.
- the gamma is incorrect and has poor calibration (slide gamma all the way to the right and you can ACTUALLY SEE THE GAME)
- awful audio balancing (slide music down to 0 and half voice files, special FX and skill sounds dissapear, wtf are they in the wrong categories?)
- the herb mechanic (why not let us i dont know GATHER them to use later and quickslot them? 
Instead they heal instantly on the spot like a hand holding simulator, mid combat but you have to spend 1 minute with arrows flying at you to carefully and methodically cut the herb... (the lore within the game says some plants cant be collected without special gloves AND YET)
- the stealth quests are among the worst ive ever experienced, you blink and the quest fails
- when you accidentally step outside a quest area - instant failure of quest :S (this is enough to make me hate most games that implement this)
- not to mention over a dozen quests have the insufferable TIME LIMIT or fail ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥
- with weapons half the runes dont even bloody work - literally go test for yourselves
- the dominate/brand command is so useless, you command them to do something but YOU have to do it LMAO, why bother adding that mechanic at all?
- forced sheathing of weapons which get in the way of dodging/climbing efficiently
- the wraith/elven speed is so poorly implemented it actually hinders gameplay ironically
- stealth is rubbish, instant quest failures because you chose to use a ranged stealth skill instead of melee and yet THE QUEST SAYS USE STEALTH SKILLS
- the developers changed the lore to suit their own tastes in these games - WHY? 

When you can actually get the game to do what you want, then the combat is fun. The branding is cool, Nemesis is overrated and illogical.

The story is terrible - may as well watch Bold and the Beautiful and name all the characters in Elven/Dwarven, they changed the lore (wtf is that ending?), the cinematics are 5 pixels so you can't even see the characters.

Just no.

4/10

Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor is a game that I had a great time with, especially because I’m already a fan of The Lord of the Rings movies and the world they created. The combat feels incredibly fun and satisfying, and I really liked having the freedom to approach encounters however I wanted, whether through stealth or by charging straight into a fight. The map is not particularly large and exploration can be a bit limited, but somehow it never felt boring to roam around Mordor, hunting Orc captains and causing chaos. I was also impressed by how good the graphics still look while running very smoothly on my system. Overall, I enjoyed almost every moment of it and would easily recommend it to anyone who likes action games or the Middle-earth universe.
Game did not age well.  I may have liked it at one time but just not overly fun killing the same thing over and over and over.  Combat is overly simple as well.
This game is fantastic. I'm not even a lotr fan, I just love stabbing absolute ♥♥♥♥ out of the orc ♥♥♥♥♥.
